title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Nasi eksperci w podcaście omawiają, jak rozpoznawać restrukturyzację z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen oraz jakie obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) i ryzyka podatkowe mogą się z tym wiązać. Podkreślają znaczenie terminowej dokumentacji, roli wycen, najczęstszych błędów oraz dają praktyczną checklistę działań przy przenoszeniu produkcji, dystrybucji, pracowników czy kontraktów.
bullets: Czym jest restrukturyzacja w kontekście cen transferowych i jak ją identyfikować.
Jak rozpoznać istotną zmianę operacyjną (analiza EBIT) i jej konsekwencje.
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji oraz korzyści z jej przygotowania „na czas”.
Obowiązki raportowe związane ze zmianami (TPR, MDR).
Rola wycen, typowe błędy i praktyczne przykłady (przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników/kontraktów).
takeaways: Przy planowaniu przeniesienia operacji (np. centrum danych, infrastruktury IT) uwzględnij implikacje cen transferowych i potrzeby wyceny.
Zadbaj o kompletność i terminowość dokumentacji restrukturyzacyjnej, aby ograniczyć ryzyko podatkowe i przyszłe komplikacje.
Zweryfikuj obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) przy zmianach transgranicznych lub przy istotnej zmianie profilu działalności.
Zaangażuj ekspertów ds. wycen i cen transferowych przed transferem pracowników lub kontraktów, by uniknąć powszechnych błędów wyceny.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Marcus Oliver, lead of Google Delta (Google’s professional services team), says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy—not computing costs. He recommends reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most costly AI mistake: jumping into code without a clear strategy.
Computing costs are not the primary expense highlighted.
Emphasis on reimagining value chains to unlock AI value.
Recommend deploying multi-agent systems to achieve real outcomes.
Presented by Marcus Oliver of Google Delta on The AI Vantage.
takeaways: Prioritize strategic planning over immediate investment in compute resources.
Align AI initiatives with reimagined value chains to maximize ROI.
Consider infrastructure and operational support for multi-agent system deployments.
Engage professional services or strategy teams early to avoid the 'coding trap.'

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This re-release of the kickoff episode from PwC’s Reporting reset series reviews foundational financial statement presentation principles, key disclosure considerations, differences between public and private company reporting, and guidance on accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events. The episode includes timestamps for each topic and links to PwC’s Financial Statement Presentation guide chapters for deeper reading.
bullets: Covers GAAP and SEC requirements for financial statement presentation (1:25).
Guidance on selecting appropriate reporting periods (3:09).
Balance sheet and income statement presentation, required disclosures, and best practices (5:25; 11:44).
Discussion of accounting changes, estimates, error corrections (21:31).
Treatment and disclosure of subsequent events (29:53) and links to relevant PwC guide chapters.
takeaways: Ensure balance sheet and income statement classifications and disclosures are complete to support transparent financial reporting and stakeholder review.
Confirm reporting period choices and presentation structures early in the close process to reduce restatements or disclosure issues.
Have processes to identify, evaluate, and disclose accounting changes, estimate revisions, and error corrections to limit surprises at year-end.
Track and disclose subsequent events timely, as they can affect reported results and stakeholder decision-making.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform designed to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility, policy enforcement, and real-time protections. It highlights three core capabilities—AI Lens for visibility and policies, AI Red Teaming to stress-test models and apps, and an AI Firewall to secure prompts and responses—aimed at eliminating shadow AI and protecting infrastructure from evolving threats.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across AI user interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time guardrails for prompts and responses.
Focus on eliminating shadow AI and protecting infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Total visibility and policy controls are essential to identify and eliminate shadow AI risks.
Regular red teaming should be adopted to validate resilience of models and applications against real-world attacks.
Real-time prompt/response controls can help prevent unsafe or unauthorized AI interactions that threaten infrastructure.
A unified security approach across agents and apps reduces gaps between development and operations for AI deployments.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest: u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The description lists the full winning system specs, the GeForce host (Dwight), and links to the PCPartPicker build and NVIDIA social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam with an SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 AIO.
Host: GeForce Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
PCPartPicker link provided for the full parts list.
takeaways: High-end GPUs like the RTX 5090 are being integrated into small-form-factor systems—plan for thermal and spatial constraints when deploying powerful cards.
A 1000W SFX power supply (Corsair SF1000) is used in this build, highlighting significant power provisioning needs even in compact systems.
The build pairs a high-performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) and DDR5-6000 RAM with the GPU, underscoring the importance of balanced system components for maximum performance.
